You are a mental health triage assistant. Analyze the person's message carefully and respond ONLY with a valid JSON object using this exact schema:
{
  "severity": "crisis | high | moderate | low",
  "concern_type": "suicidal_ideation | self_harm | depression | anxiety | panic | ptsd | grief | burnout | loneliness | eating_disorder | substance_abuse | general_distress",
  "recommended_action": "emergency_services | immediate_therapist | scheduled_therapist | self_help_resources | peer_support | monitoring",
  "risk_flags": ["only flags explicitly present in the message"],
  "empathetic_opening": "One warm validating sentence specific to this person's situation",
  "follow_up_question": "One clarifying question to better understand their needs"
}
Rules: crisis → emergency_services or immediate_therapist only. No markdown. No explanation. JSON only.
I’ve been feeling really drained lately at the office. My usual spark is gone, and I’m finding it hard to focus on projects. Even after a full night’s sleep, I’m still exhausted.

{
  "severity": "low",
  "concern_type": "burnout",
  "recommended_action": "self_help_resources",
  "risk_flags": [
    "fatigue",
    "concentration_difficulty"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "It sounds like your energy has been low at work and you\u2019re struggling to focus.",
  "follow_up_question": "Can you tell me more about what your typical workday looks like and any recent changes in your workload?"
}

I'm really nervous about my upcoming exams, I've been studying for weeks but I still feel like I'm not prepared. I just wish I could get through this first exam period without too much stress. I've been having some trouble sleeping because I keep thinking about all the things that could go wrong.

I've been stuck at home for weeks, too scared to leave my house because I'm terrified of having a panic attack in public. I've tried to go out a few times, but I just can't shake the feeling that something awful will happen. I'm starting to feel like I'm losing my mind and I don't know how much more of this I can take. I just want to be able to go to the grocery store without having a meltdown.

{
  "severity": "high",
  "concern_type": "panic",
  "recommended_action": "immediate_therapist",
  "risk_flags": [
    "isolation",
    "hopelessness"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "It sounds like being stuck at home for weeks has been really overwhelming for you and you're feeling like you're losing control.",
  "follow_up_question": "Can you tell me more about what's been holding you back from seeking help or leaving your house, is it the panic attacks themselves or something else?"
}

I just can't take this anymore, I've been studying for my exams non-stop and I feel like I'm drowning in stress. I cut myself again last night because it was the only way I could release the pressure. I'm so scared and alone, and I don't know how to make it stop. My exams are in a week and I just want it all to be over.

{
  "severity": "crisis",
  "concern_type": "self_harm",
  "recommended_action": "emergency_services",
  "risk_flags": [
    "self_harm_mention",
    "hopelessness"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "I can see that the exam pressure has become overwhelming for you, and it's completely understandable to feel like you're drowning in stress.",
  "follow_up_question": "Can you tell me more about what happened last night and how you're feeling right now?"
}

I’ve been working 12‑hour days for the past few months at my tech job, and I’m just completely exhausted. I can’t seem to get a good night’s sleep, and even small things at work make me snap. I feel like I’m losing my spark and don’t enjoy anything outside of work anymore.

{
  "severity": "moderate",
  "concern_type": "burnout",
  "recommended_action": "scheduled_therapist",
  "risk_flags": [
    "fatigue",
    "irritability",
    "sleep_disruption"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "It sounds incredibly draining to keep up those 12\u2011hour days and still feel wiped out.",
  "follow_up_question": "Can you tell me more about how your work schedule has been affecting your sleep and mood?"
}

I'm 52 and my divorce just finalized. I feel like I've lost everything that mattered - my family, my home, and now I'm struggling to make ends meet. Some days I just wonder if it's all worth it, you know? Like, what's the point of keeping going? I don't think I could actually do anything to hurt myself, but the thought is there, lingering. It's scary to admit, but it feels like a weight is crushing me.

I'm a complete failure, I haven't been able to get out of bed for weeks, let alone go to work. I used to be a high-powered executive, but now I feel like I'm just going through the motions of life. I've lost all interest in things I used to love and I just feel so worthless all the time.

{
  "severity": "high",
  "concern_type": "depression",
  "recommended_action": "immediate_therapist",
  "risk_flags": [
    "worthlessness",
    "anhedonia",
    "sleep_disruption",
    "isolation"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "It sounds like the pressure of being a high-powered executive has become overwhelming and is affecting your ability to enjoy the things that brought you joy.",
  "follow_up_question": "Can you tell me more about what's been going on at work that's making it so difficult for you to get out of bed in the morning?"
}

I just feel like I'm not good enough and I'm going to fail. I've been lying awake at night worrying about it all.

{
  "severity": "moderate",

}

I'm completely numb and can't stop thinking about my husband, he passed away 3 days ago. I don't know how I'm supposed to keep going without him. Sometimes I think it would just be easier if I wasn't here anymore. I feel like I'm drowning in this sadness and I don't know how to keep my head above water.

{
  "severity": "high",
  "concern_type": "suicidal_ideation",
  "recommended_action": "immediate_therapist",
  "risk_flags": [
    "passive_suicidal_ideation",
    "recent_loss"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "I can only imagine how overwhelming this must feel, losing your husband so suddenly and having to navigate this dark time without him by your side.",
  "follow_up_question": "What kind of support system do you have in place right now, and have you considered reaching out to friends, family, or a support group for help?"
}
